---
title: "Final Review"
documenttype: Handout
author:
  - name: Saaleh Poovathumkadavil
toc: true

version: [v.1.0]
date: last-modified
execute: 
  echo: fenced
  freeze: auto
bibliography: references.bib
bibliographystyle: "springer-humanities-author-date" 
format:
  tufte-inspired-html: default
---


# Basic R

```{r echo=TRUE}
a <- c(2, 3, 5, 7, 11)
rep(a, times = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5))
round(seq(from=1, to=11, length.out=15))
mtcars[3,6]
great_lakes <- data.frame(
  name = c("Huron", "Ontario", "Michigan", "Erie", "Superior"),
  volume = c(3500, 1640, 4900, 480, 12000), # km^3
  max_depth = c(228, 245, 282, 64, 406) # meters
)
str(great_lakes)
b <- c(2, 3, NA, 2, NA)
b[!is.na(b)]
length(unique(b))
```

# Probability

An experiment produces observations. An outcome is a possible observation.
The set of all possible outcomes is the sample space. An event is a subset of
the sample space. A trial is a single running of the experiment.

```{=typst}
#v(1em)
```

**Example 2.2** Roll a die and observe the number of dots on the top face.
This is an expiriment, with six possible outcomes. The sample space is the set
$S=\{1,2,3,4,5,6\}$. The event "roll higher than 3" is the set $\{4,5,6\}$.
